requirements: The core contributions of the paper '3D-Flow: Hybrid-Bonded 3D NPU Co-Design for FlashAttention' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The simulation infrastructure and environment have been set up.
Code has been implemented to integrate Ramulator (or Ramulator 2.0) for modeling DRAM access latency and bandwidth.

verification.spec: Verify the simulator imports a Ramulator (or Ramulator 2.0) Python/C++ binding module and that the DRAM subsystem of the simulator delegates latency/bandwidth queries to Ramulator. Inspect the simulator's DRAM model class and confirm it holds a Ramulator handle and forwards memory requests to it.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to integrate Accelergy for energy estimation based on tracked hardware activity counts.

verification.spec: Run a short simulation and confirm the simulator emits an Accelergy-compatible action-counts file (YAML/JSON listing component activity counts) and invokes Accelergy to produce an energy estimate. Check that the energy report contains component-level energies (MAC, Reg, SRAM, DRAM).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for a double-buffering mechanism between memory levels to hide access latency in the simulator.

verification.spec: Construct a small workload where memory access latency exceeds compute latency for a single tile, and run the simulator with double-buffering enabled vs disabled. Verify that with double-buffering, the simulator overlaps load of tile N+1 with compute of tile N (i.e., total cycles ~= max(compute, load) per tile rather than sum), confirmed via cycle counts in simulator logs.

sub_tasks.requirements: RTL implementation of the complete four-tier PE array has been synthesized with Synopsys Design Compiler on TSMC 16nm technology, and synthesized energy/area numbers are used to calibrate the simulator energy model.

verification.spec: Verify the repository contains Verilog/SystemVerilog RTL for all four PE tier types and a top-level 4-tier array, a Synopsys DC synthesis script targeting TSMC 16nm, and synthesis reports (area/power) for each tier. Confirm the simulator's per-PE energy constants reference these synthesis numbers via a documented calibration file.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Z-axis (inter-tier register-to-register) data transfer energy is conservatively set to 1.35 pJ/byte in the energy model.

verification.spec: Open the simulator/energy configuration file and confirm the Z-axis (TSV / inter-tier register) data transfer energy is set to exactly 1.35 pJ/byte. Also confirm it is consumed by the inter-tier transfer model in the simulator code path.

sub_tasks.requirements: A Timeloop-based cost model with linear pruned search strategy has been implemented to find optimal tiling/mapping schemes for the 2D-Unfused and 3D-Base baselines.

verification.spec: Invoke the mapping search on a small attention problem (e.g., seq_len=1K, d=128) for both the 2D-Unfused and 3D-Base baselines. Verify the search uses a Timeloop-based cost model (Timeloop binding/import present) and a linear pruned search strategy (e.g., pruning factor or beam parameter), and returns a tiling/mapping with deterministic, lower cost than a random mapping baseline.

sub_tasks.requirements: The 3D-Flow hardware architecture has been implemented.
A 3D-stacked PE array with 4 vertically stacked tiers (Layer 0–Layer 3) has been implemented, each tier being a 128x128 array configured for d=128 head dimension.

verification.spec: Instantiate the 3D-Flow architecture and inspect its configuration object: confirm there are exactly 4 tiers labelled Layer 0–3, each tier is a 128x128 PE array, and that d=128 is the head dimension used for tile sizing. Assert the total PE count equals 4*128*128.

sub_tasks.requirements: Adjacent compute tiers are integrated face-to-back, with registers of spatially aligned PEs interconnected via hybrid-bonded TSV links providing single-cycle data forwarding between tiers.

verification.spec: Trace a single value written into an intermediate register of a PE at coordinate (i,j) in Layer k. Verify it is visible to the spatially aligned PE (i,j) in Layer k+1 in the next cycle (single-cycle forwarding) via the TSV link model, and that no SRAM access is logged for that transfer.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 0 PE microarchitecture has been implemented containing MAC units configured with an Outer-Stationary (OS) dataflow to execute the QK^T computation.

verification.spec: Feed deterministic Q and K tiles into Layer 0 and run the layer's QK^T pipeline. Verify (a) the PE microarchitecture uses an Outer-Stationary dataflow (output S element stays in PE until complete, Q and K stream through), (b) S(0,0) is produced after d cycles and S is fully produced after 3d cycles, and (c) the resulting S matrix matches numpy Q @ K.T within fp tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 1 PE microarchitecture has been implemented containing comparator modules and adders (configured for subtraction) to compute RowMax and perform subtraction (producing N and a).

verification.spec: Drive Layer 1 with a known S matrix (and a prior old_m vector). Verify the layer outputs S_max per row matching numpy rowmax(S), new_m = max(old_m, S_max), a = old_m - new_m, and N = S - new_m elementwise. Confirm S_max is ready at 2d cycles after first S arrival and a, N at 3d and 4d cycles respectively.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 2 PE microarchitecture has been implemented containing exponential (exp2) computation units, multipliers, and adders for exp and RowSum operators, with the constant log2(e)/sqrt(d) stored in each PE register.

verification.spec: Drive Layer 2 with N and a inputs. Verify (a) each PE's register holds the constant log2(e)/sqrt(d) at initialization, (b) the PE first multiplies N (or a) by this constant and then applies exp2, producing P = exp2(N*log2(e)/sqrt(d)) and b = exp2(a*log2(e)/sqrt(d)) that match numpy exp(N/sqrt(d)) and exp(a/sqrt(d)), and (c) local_l = rowsum(P) and new_l = old_l*b + local_l match the reference.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 3 PE microarchitecture has been implemented containing MAC units with a reverse data path to implement PV and the O scaling operation, configured with Weight-Stationary (WS) dataflow for PV.

verification.spec: Drive Layer 3 with P (from TSV), V tile, b, and old_O. Verify (a) V tile is fed from the left in a parallelogram pattern with Weight-Stationary dataflow (P stays resident), (b) partial sums are accumulated vertically downward and local_O is produced at the last row at 3d cycles, (c) the reverse path forwards local_O to the first row to compute new_O = diag(b)*old_O + local_O, completing at 5d cycles, and (d) outputs match numpy P @ V plus scaling.

sub_tasks.requirements: The 3D-FlashAttention dataflow scheduling has been implemented.

FlashAttention-2 forward pass (Algorithm 1) has been implemented, including the tiled inner loop with online softmax updates (oldm, oldl, oldO, newm, b, P, locall, newl, localO, newO) and final O_i = diag(oldl)^-1 * oldO scaling.

verification.spec: Run the full FlashAttention-2 forward pass on the implementation with random Q, K, V of moderate size (e.g., LEN=512, d=64). Verify (a) the tiled inner loop maintains and updates the running state (old_m, old_l, old_O) using the formulas in Algorithm 1, (b) the final O_i = diag(old_l)^-1 * old_O scaling is applied, and (c) the output matches a reference PyTorch softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) @ V to within 1e-3 relative error.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 0 dataflow has been implemented: Q tile and K tile are fed into the systolic array in a parallelogram injection pattern; S(0,0) is produced after d cycles and forwarded to Layer 1 via TSV; the next QK^T iteration can begin after 2d cycles.

verification.spec: Run a unit test that feeds known Q tile and K tile (d=128) into the Layer 0 systolic-array simulator/RTL. Verify (a) parallelogram injection pattern timing, (b) S(0,0) emerges at cycle d and is signaled on the TSV output to Layer 1, (c) all S elements completed by 3d cycles, (d) a fresh QK^T iteration can be launched at cycle 2d (top-left PE becomes idle).

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 1 dataflow has been implemented: S propagates rightward to compute row-wise S_max by 2d cycles, then S_max propagates leftward and is compared with old_m to produce new_m, then subtraction yields N (matrix) and a (vector), completed at 4d and 3d cycles respectively.

verification.spec: Feed a synthetic S matrix into the Layer 1 simulator. Verify (a) rightward propagation produces row-wise S_max by cycle 2d, (b) leftward pass compares with old_m to produce new_m, (c) subtraction emits N matrix (completed at 4d) and a vector (completed at 3d, rightmost column), (d) N and a are emitted onto the TSV link to Layer 2 cycle-accurately.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 2 dataflow has been implemented: N/a is multiplied with log2(e)/sqrt(d), passed to exp2 unit producing P/b; P is forwarded to Layer 3 via TSV and also propagates leftward for accumulation into local_l; local_l multiply-accumulates with b and old_l to produce new_l.

verification.spec: Test Layer 2 PE behavior: provide N/a inputs, verify that each PE multiplies by stored constant log2(e)/sqrt(d), passes through exp2 unit to produce P (matrix) and b (vector), then (a) P is forwarded vertically to Layer 3 via TSV, (b) P propagates leftward to accumulate into local_l, and (c) local_l is combined with b*old_l to compute new_l = old_l*b + local_l. Compare numerical output against a reference softmax computation.

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer 3 dataflow has been implemented: V tile is injected from the left in a parallelogram pattern starting at 2d cycle, multiplied with P from TSV under Weight-Stationary dataflow with vertical partial-sum accumulation; local_O is produced at 3d cycle and combined with old_O and diag(b) to produce new_O by 5d cycle.

verification.spec: Feed V tile and P (from TSV) into Layer 3 simulator. Verify (a) V injected from the left in parallelogram pattern starting at cycle 2d, (b) Weight-Stationary dataflow with downward vertical partial-sum accumulation, (c) local_O appears at cycle 3d, (d) local_O is multiply-accumulated with diag(b)*old_O to form new_O, (e) all completed by cycle 5d, (f) results emerge from the top of the array.

sub_tasks.requirements: The bubble-free vertical pipeline has been implemented such that one full inner-loop iteration of FlashAttention completes every 2d cycles when the pipeline is fully filled, with all intermediate results transferred via TSV register-to-register links and no SRAM round-trips between operators.

verification.spec: Run multiple consecutive inner-loop iterations through the 4-tier pipeline simulator and measure the per-iteration steady-state cycle count. Verify the cycle count equals 2d once the pipeline is filled (d=128 -> 256 cycles/iteration). Inspect SRAM access logs for inter-operator intermediates (S, N, a, P, b, local_O) and verify they are zero (all transfers occur via TSV register links).

sub_tasks.requirements: Post-inner-loop O scaling by old_l (Algorithm 1 line 21) has been implemented, and multiple attention heads can be processed in parallel by integrating multiple 3D-stacked PE arrays.

verification.spec: Run a full FlashAttention pass through the simulator and verify after the inner loop completes that an additional scaling step computes O_i = diag(old_l)^-1 * old_O (Algorithm 1 line 21). Then instantiate 2+ 3D-stacked arrays in the simulator config and confirm multiple attention heads are dispatched concurrently with linear throughput scaling.

sub_tasks.requirements: The baseline accelerators have been implemented for comparison.

2D-Unfused baseline has been implemented as a 128x128 conventional systolic array (4 clusters) executing attention phases sequentially without operator fusion, with mapping found via the Timeloop-based pruned search.

verification.spec: Verify the simulator config for 2D-Unfused: 4 clusters of 128x128 systolic arrays, 60MB on-chip memory, 8 TB/s on-chip BW, 400 GB/s off-chip BW, no operator fusion (QK^T, softmax, PV executed as separate kernels with SRAM round-trips). Run the Timeloop-based pruned mapping search and dump the chosen tiling; execute an OPT-13B attention layer and verify output numerical correctness against a CPU reference.

sub_tasks.requirements: FuseMax 2D-Fused baseline has been implemented, performing deep operator fusion on a single 128x128 array with rowmax/rowsum as spatial reductions and pipelined FlashAttention iterations using 10 registers per PE.

verification.spec: Instantiate the FuseMax baseline on a 128x128 array and verify (a) each PE provides 10 intermediate registers for context-switching across pipelined iterations, (b) rowmax and rowsum are computed as spatial reductions across the array (not by an external SFU), (c) multiple FlashAttention iterations are concurrently pipelined on the array, and (d) end-to-end attention output matches reference numpy.

sub_tasks.requirements: FLAT 2D-Fused baseline has been implemented as an optimized fused dataflow on a single 128x128 array.

verification.spec: Instantiate the FLAT baseline on a single 128x128 array and verify it runs the FLAT optimized fused attention dataflow end-to-end on a small attention workload producing output that matches reference numpy attention, and that its mapping/dataflow parameters match the FLAT paper's description.

sub_tasks.requirements: TileFlow 2D-Fused baseline has been implemented for fusion dataflow on a single 128x128 array.

verification.spec: Instantiate the TileFlow baseline on a single 128x128 array, run a small attention workload, and verify the simulator drives the array with TileFlow's tree-based fusion dataflow producing attention output matching reference numpy.

sub_tasks.requirements: Dual-SA baseline (inspired by COSA) has been implemented as a 2D dual-array architecture (128x128x2, 2 clusters) that partitions QK^T and PV onto two arrays connected by a dedicated Softmax SFU unit, bypassing on-chip SRAM between the compute units.

verification.spec: Instantiate the Dual-SA baseline and verify (a) it has two 128x128 PE arrays in 2 clusters with a dedicated Softmax SFU between them, (b) QK^T executes on array 1 and PV on array 2, (c) intermediate S/P values flow directly between arrays via the SFU without writing to the main on-chip SRAM (verified by zero SRAM accesses for these intermediates in the simulator log), and (d) end-to-end attention output matches reference numpy.

sub_tasks.requirements: 3D-Base baseline has been implemented with the same 128x128x4 stacked architecture as 3D-Flow but mapping each operator onto stacked tiers per prior 3D-DNN methods (with intermediate results exchanged via SRAM rather than registers).

verification.spec: Instantiate the 3D-Base baseline with the same 128x128x4 stacked physical architecture as 3D-Flow. Verify that (a) each operator is mapped per the cited 3D-DNN methods (refs [30],[31]), and (b) inter-operator intermediates are written to and read from on-chip SRAM (non-zero SRAM access counts for these intermediates in the simulator log), distinguishing it from 3D-Flow's register-only path.

sub_tasks.requirements: All baselines and the proposed design are configured with equivalent compute resources, 60MB on-chip memory, 8 TB/s on-chip BW, and 400 GB/s off-chip BW per Table I.

verification.spec: Load each accelerator configuration (Ours, 3D-Base, 2D-Unfused, 2D-Fused, Dual-SA) and assert: (a) total PE count is equivalent (e.g., 128*128*4 across all designs via clustering), (b) on-chip memory size = 60 MB, (c) on-chip bandwidth = 8 TB/s, (d) off-chip bandwidth = 400 GB/s, matching Table I.

sub_tasks.requirements: Benchmark workloads have been prepared.
OPT-13B model configuration (Multi-Head Attention) has been set up for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load the OPT-13B Multi-Head Attention config (40 layers, 40 heads, head_dim=128, hidden_size=5120 per published config) and verify the simulator-facing model spec exposes these dimensions and an MHA attention pattern. Smoke-test a single forward attention pass and confirm shape correctness.

sub_tasks.requirements: Qwen-13B model configuration (Grouped-Query Attention) has been set up for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load the Qwen-13B Grouped-Query Attention config and verify the simulator-facing model spec exposes head_dim, num_attention_heads, num_key_value_heads (GQA group count) and asserts num_kv_heads < num_attention_heads. Smoke-test a single attention forward pass.

sub_tasks.requirements: Dynamic RoPE scaling has been implemented to extend OPT and Qwen context windows beyond their pre-trained limits, enabling evaluation at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, and 64K.

verification.spec: Apply dynamic RoPE scaling (per LongRoPE2 or equivalent) to OPT-13B and Qwen-13B configs and verify the resulting models accept and run attention with sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K without throwing positional-encoding errors. Verify the rotary embedding tensors are correctly scaled to each length.

sub_tasks.requirements: Evaluation experiments and reported numerical claims have been reproduced.

Motivational energy breakdown of operator fusion vs. unfusion for OPT at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K has been reproduced (Fig. 1), demonstrating on-chip SRAM accesses account for over 60% of total energy in long-sequence (N>2k) FlashAttention workloads.

verification.spec: Reproduce Fig. 1: run the simulator on OPT attention with operator fusion (FlashAttention-style) and without fusion at seq_len = 1K, 4K, 16K. Tally energy by category (MAC, Reg, SRAM, DRAM) using Accelergy. Verify the SRAM share exceeds 60% of total energy for the fused case at seq_len >= 4K.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced energy consumption shows Ours reduces energy by 80.5% to 93% compared to 2D-Unfused (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run the attention workload for OPT-13B and QWEN-13B at seq_len in {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K} on Ours and on 2D-Unfused. Compute energy reduction = 1 - E_Ours / E_2D-Unfused for each (model, seq_len) pair. Verify the minimum reduction across all 8 points is >= 80.5% and maximum is <= 93%, and the mean falls within tolerance of 86.75%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced energy consumption shows Ours saves 54.2% to 66.7% energy compared to FuseMax and Dual-SA (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run the attention workload for OPT-13B and QWEN-13B at seq_len in {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K} on Ours, FuseMax, and Dual-SA. Compute energy reduction = 1 - E_Ours / E_baseline for each baseline. Verify the reduction range across all points and both baselines is [54.2%, 66.7%] with mean within tolerance of 60.45%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced energy consumption shows Ours achieves an average energy advantage of 46.8% over 3D-Base (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run attention workloads for OPT-13B and QWEN-13B at seq_len in {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K} on Ours and 3D-Base. Compute average energy reduction = 1 - mean(E_Ours)/mean(E_3D-Base) across all 8 points. Verify the average matches 46.8% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Memory data-movement decomposition (Fig. 6) into DRAM and on-chip memory has been reproduced for all baselines, normalized to 2D-Unfused.

verification.spec: From the simulator memory-traffic logs across all baselines, compute per-design DRAM bytes and on-chip SRAM bytes, normalize to 2D-Unfused, and plot the stacked-bar (Fig. 6). Verify FuseMax shows ~85.5% DRAM reduction and ~2.1x on-chip SRAM increase vs 2D-Unfused.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced results confirm FuseMax reduces DRAM accesses by 85.5% but increases on-chip SRAM traffic by 2.1x relative to 2D-Unfused (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run the attention workload on FuseMax and 2D-Unfused (averaged over the evaluated seq_lens / models per Figure 6). Compute (a) DRAM access reduction = 1 - DRAM_FuseMax / DRAM_2D-Unfused (verify ~85.5% within tolerance), and (b) on-chip SRAM traffic ratio = SRAM_FuseMax / SRAM_2D-Unfused (verify ~2.1x within tolerance). Report the DRAM reduction as the measured value.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced results confirm Ours reduces on-chip SRAM traffic by an average of 76.6% compared to other fusion baselines (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run the cycle-accurate simulator on OPT-13B and Qwen-13B at sequence lengths 1K/4K/16K/64K for Ours and the fusion baselines (FuseMax, FLAT, TileFlow, Dual-SA, 3D-Base). Extract on-chip SRAM access counts (bytes) per workload. Compute the average percentage reduction of Ours SRAM traffic relative to the mean of the fusion baselines across all (model, seq_len) configurations and verify it is ~76.6%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced results confirm Ours reduces overall (end-to-end) inference energy by 32.7% to 64.2% on average compared to baselines (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run end-to-end inference energy simulation of Ours and each baseline on OPT-13B/Qwen-13B across seq_lens; compute the relative energy reduction of Ours vs each baseline averaged across configs. Verify the spread of mean reductions is in [32.7%, 64.2%].

sub_tasks.requirements: Energy breakdown of Ours (Table II) by component (MAC, Reg, SRAM, DRAM, 3D-IC Overhead) has been reproduced for sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, 64K.
At seq_len=1K, reproduced breakdown matches MAC=8.5%, Reg=21.2%, SRAM=38.3%, DRAM=26.7%, 3D-IC Overhead=5.3% (with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run Ours at seq_len=1K and extract per-component energy fractions: MAC, Reg, SRAM, DRAM, 3D-IC Overhead. Verify the breakdown matches MAC=8.5%, Reg=21.2%, SRAM=38.3%, DRAM=26.7%, 3D-IC=5.3% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: At seq_len=4K, reproduced breakdown matches MAC=11.7%, Reg=31.9%, SRAM=35.0%, DRAM=15.1%, 3D-IC Overhead=6.3% (with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run Ours at seq_len=4K and extract per-component energy fractions. Verify MAC=11.7%, Reg=31.9%, SRAM=35.0%, DRAM=15.1%, 3D-IC=6.3% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: At seq_len=16K, reproduced breakdown matches MAC=10.4%, Reg=29.2%, SRAM=29.5%, DRAM=20.8%, 3D-IC Overhead=10.1% (with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run Ours at seq_len=16K and extract per-component energy fractions. Verify MAC=10.4%, Reg=29.2%, SRAM=29.5%, DRAM=20.8%, 3D-IC=10.1% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: At seq_len=64K, reproduced breakdown matches MAC=12.0%, Reg=34.4%, SRAM=28.5%, DRAM=16.2%, 3D-IC Overhead=8.9% (with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run Ours at seq_len=64K and extract per-component energy fractions. Verify MAC=12.0%, Reg=34.4%, SRAM=28.5%, DRAM=16.2%, 3D-IC=8.9% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced 3D-IC technology energy overhead averages 7.81% (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: From the energy breakdown table for Ours (Table II), sum the '3D-IC Overhead' column across seq lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, 64K and divide by 4. Verify the average overhead is ~7.81% (mean of 5.3, 6.3, 10.1, 8.9).

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced speedup of Ours vs. 2D-Unfused averages 7.62x (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run the cycle-accurate simulator for attention on OPT-13B and Qwen-13B at seq lengths 1K/4K/16K/64K for both Ours and 2D-Unfused. Extract total cycles and compute speedup = cycles(2D-Unfused)/cycles(Ours) per config; verify the arithmetic mean across all 8 configurations is ~7.62x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced speedup of Ours vs. FuseMax averages 1.46x (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: From the simulator cycle results, compute cycles(FuseMax)/cycles(Ours) for each (model, seq_len) in {OPT-13B, Qwen-13B} x {1K,4K,16K,64K}; verify the mean is ~1.46x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced speedup of Ours vs. Dual-SA averages 2.36x (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: From the simulator cycle results, compute cycles(Dual-SA)/cycles(Ours) per (model, seq_len) configuration; verify the mean is ~2.36x.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced speedup of Ours vs. 3D-Base averages 1.43x (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: Run the cycle-accurate simulator for both Ours and 3D-Base configurations (128x128x4 array, 60MB on-chip, 8 TB/s on-chip BW, 400 GB/s off-chip BW) across OPT-13B and Qwen-13B at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, 64K. Compute speedup = latency(3D-Base) / latency(Ours) for each config and average across all configs. Assert the mean is within tolerance of 1.43x.

sub_tasks.requirements: PE utilization experiments (Fig. 8) have been run for all designs at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K.

verification.spec: Run the PE utilization experiment script and verify it produces utilization numbers for every baseline (2D-Unfused, FLAT, TileFlow, FuseMax, Dual-SA, 3D-Base, Ours) at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K. Check that the output table/figure data contains 7 designs x 6 sequence lengths = 42 non-null utilization values.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced average PE utilization of Ours is approximately 87% (numerical claim with tolerance).

verification.spec: From simulator logs of Ours across seq lengths 1K, 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K (Fig. 8 setup), extract per-cycle active PE count divided by total PEs (128*128*4) and average over time and configurations. Verify the mean PE utilization is ~0.87.

sub_tasks.requirements: Result analysis confirms the qualitative conclusions of the paper.
Analysis confirms that on-chip SRAM accesses become the dominant energy bottleneck in fused FlashAttention workloads with long sequences on 2D accelerators.

verification.spec: Reproduce Figure 1 energy breakdown by running the simulator on OPT in fused FlashAttention mode at sequence length 16K on a 2D accelerator config. Sum SRAM access energy and divide by total energy. Assert SRAM fraction > 0.60.

sub_tasks.requirements: Analysis confirms 3D-Flow achieves a complete inner-loop iteration in 2d cycles when pipelined, compared to 3d cycles required by a conventional 2D PE array merely for QK^T.

verification.spec: Inspect the 3D-Flow dataflow simulator / RTL trace for one inner-loop iteration at array dimension d=128. Verify that after pipeline fill, a new iteration completes every 2*d=256 cycles (steady-state throughput). Separately verify that on a 2D systolic baseline of the same d, the QK^T operator alone takes 3*d=384 cycles. Assert the 2d vs 3d cycle counts match.

sub_tasks.requirements: Analysis confirms 3D-Flow reduces 46–93% energy consumption and achieves 1.4×–7.6× speedups compared to state-of-the-art 2D and 3D designs across the evaluated Transformer workloads.

verification.spec: Run the full evaluation on OPT-13B and Qwen-13B at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, 64K, comparing Ours vs. all baselines (2D-Unfused, FLAT, TileFlow, FuseMax, Dual-SA, 3D-Base). Compute (a) energy reduction = 1 - E(Ours)/E(baseline) and (b) speedup = latency(baseline)/latency(Ours). Assert min energy reduction >= 0.46 and max >= 0.93 (with tolerance), and min speedup >= 1.4x and max speedup >= 7.6x (with tolerance).

sub_tasks.requirements: Additional coverage added by critique pass
Numerical claims from paper tables/figures

Code/configuration documents that hybrid-bonded TSV interconnects use sub-10 µm pitch with one-cycle latency, while traditional micro-bump interconnects use 40-50 µm pitch, and this is reflected in the simulator's interconnect modeling parameters.

verification.spec: Inspect simulator/config files for hybrid-bonded TSV pitch parameter <=10 µm and latency=1 cycle; verify a micro-bump baseline parameter in 40-50 µm range is also defined for comparison

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the energy model uses an SRAM access energy that is 10-20x the FMA energy (Horowitz reference), matching the motivation in the paper.

verification.spec: Load Accelergy/energy model config; compute ratio = E_sram_access / E_fma; assert 10 <= ratio <= 20

sub_tasks.requirements: RTL synthesis of a single 128x128 PE array layer yields an estimated area of approximately 80 mm^2 on TSMC 16nm.

verification.spec: Run Synopsys Design Compiler on the RTL of one 128x128 PE array layer (TSMC 16nm); parse area report; assert |area_mm2 - 80| / 80 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Per-PE peak power is modeled/synthesized at approximately 200 µW.

verification.spec: Synthesize one PE (TSMC 16nm) and extract peak power from power report; assert |P_PE_uW - 200| / 200 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: A single 128x128 PE-array layer consumes approximately 3.3 W at peak activity.

verification.spec: Compute P_layer = 128*128 * P_PE; assert |P_layer - 3.3 W| / 3.3 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: The four-layer 3D-Flow stack total power is approximately 13.1 W.

verification.spec: Compute P_total = 4 * P_layer (+ TSV/3D-IC overhead); assert |P_total - 13.1 W| / 13.1 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Per-layer power density is approximately 41 W/cm^2.

verification.spec: Compute rho = P_layer / A_cm2 with A=80 mm^2=0.80 cm^2 and P_layer=3.3 W; assert |rho - 41| / 41 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: First-order thermal resistance model yields an internal temperature rise of approximately 2.8°C across the stack.

verification.spec: Implement first-order vertical thermal resistance model with the paper's assumed parameters; compute internal delta T; assert |dT - 2.8 C| / 2.8 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Computed junction temperature T_j at 25°C ambient is approximately 83°C.

verification.spec: Compute T_j = T_ambient + P_total * R_theta_JA + dT_internal with T_ambient=25, P_total=13.1, R=2.5, dT=2.8; assert |T_j - 83| / 83 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Cycle-accurate simulator confirms one fully-pipelined inner-loop iteration of 3D-FlashAttention completes every 2d cycles when the pipeline is full, whereas a 2D systolic array requires 3d cycles for QK^T alone.

verification.spec: Run cycle-accurate simulator with d=128 for steady-state iterations; measure cycles-per-iteration of the 3D pipeline and cycles for QK^T on 2D baseline; assert |cycles_3d_per_iter - 2*d| / (2*d) <= 0.10 and cycles_2d_QK >= 3*d * (1-0.10)

sub_tasks.requirements: Measurements show on-chip SRAM accesses account for over 60% of total energy in long-sequence (N>2k) FlashAttention workloads on a 2D fused baseline.

verification.spec: Run simulator on fused FlashAttention with OPT, seq_len in {4K, 16K}; compute SRAM_energy_fraction = E_SRAM / E_total; assert SRAM_energy_fraction >= 0.60 for at least one long-sequence config

sub_tasks.requirements: On Transformer workloads (OPT/QWEN), the 3D-Flow design reduces attention energy by up to ~93% (i.e., as low as ~7% of the 2D-Unfused baseline) for the longest sequence configurations.

verification.spec: Run attention energy evaluation for seq_len in {1K,4K,16K,64K} on OPT-13B and QWEN-13B; compute max energy reduction vs 2D-Unfused = 1 - min(E_ours/E_2DUnfused); assert |reduction - 0.93| / 0.93 <= tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the 3D-Flow accelerator achieves at least a 1.4× minimum speedup over state-of-the-art 2D and 3D baselines on Transformer attention workloads (OPT/QWEN), as measured by the cycle-accurate simulator across sequence lengths 1K-64K.

verification.spec: Run the cycle-accurate simulator for 3D-Flow and all baselines (2D-Unfused, FuseMax, FLAT, TileFlow, Dual-SA, 3D-Base) on OPT-13B and QWEN-13B attention at sequence lengths {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K}; compute min(speedup_of_ours_over_baseline) across all (baseline, seqlen, model) configurations; assert |measured_min - 1.4| / 1.4 <= 0.20

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the 3D-Flow accelerator achieves up to a 7.6× maximum speedup over the 2D-Unfused baseline on Transformer attention workloads, as measured by the cycle-accurate simulator.

verification.spec: Run the cycle-accurate simulator for 3D-Flow and 2D-Unfused on OPT-13B and QWEN-13B attention at sequence lengths {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K}; compute max(speedup_of_ours_over_2D-Unfused); assert |measured_max - 7.62| / 7.62 <= 0.20

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on-chip SRAM accesses account for over 60% of total energy in long-sequence FlashAttention workloads (N > 2K) on a conventional 2D fused baseline.

verification.spec: Run the simulator on a 2D-Fused (FuseMax-like) baseline with OPT-13B at seq_len in {4K, 16K}; compute SRAM_energy_fraction = sram_energy / total_energy; assert measured >= 0.60 with tolerance |measured - 0.65| / 0.65 <= 0.20

sub_tasks.paper_unit: fraction of total energy from SRAM

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that 3D-Flow reduces energy consumption by 80.5% to 93% compared to the 2D-Unfused baseline on attention workloads.

verification.spec: Run the simulator for 3D-Flow and 2D-Unfused on OPT-13B and QWEN-13B at seq_len in {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K}; compute energy_reduction = 1 - (E_ours / E_2D-Unfused) for each config; assert min(reduction) within tolerance of 0.805 (|x-0.805|/0.805<=0.2) and max(reduction) within tolerance of 0.93

sub_tasks.paper_unit: fractional energy reduction vs 2D-Unfused (mean of 0.805-0.93 range)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that 3D-Flow reduces on-chip SRAM traffic by an average of 76.6% compared to other fusion baselines (FuseMax, FLAT, TileFlow, Dual-SA, 3D-Base).

verification.spec: Run the simulator and collect on-chip SRAM data movement volumes for 3D-Flow and fusion baselines; compute avg_reduction = 1 - mean(sram_traffic_ours / sram_traffic_baseline) averaged over fusion baselines and sequence lengths; assert |measured - 0.766| / 0.766 <= 0.20

sub_tasks.paper_unit: fractional reduction in on-chip SRAM traffic

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that 3D-Flow achieves an average PE utilization of 87% across sequence lengths 1K to 64K.

verification.spec: Run the simulator for 3D-Flow on OPT-13B and QWEN-13B at seq_len in {1K, 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K}; compute mean PE utilization = active_PE_cycles / (total_PEs * total_cycles) averaged across configurations; assert |measured - 0.87| / 0.87 <= 0.15

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that 3D-Flow's average 3D-IC energy overhead is approximately 7.81% of total energy.

verification.spec: Run the simulator with Z-axis TSV energy modeled at 1.35 pJ/byte; for 3D-Flow on attention workloads at seq_len in {1K, 4K, 16K, 64K}, compute 3D-IC overhead = TSV_link_energy / total_energy averaged across configs; assert |measured - 0.0781| / 0.0781 <= 0.25

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the cycle-accurate simulator with Ramulator + Accelergy integration and reproduces the energy reduction of 3D-Flow vs 2D-Unfused baseline on OPT/Qwen attention workloads, achieving at least 80.5% minimum energy reduction across the sequence length sweep (1K-64K)

verification.spec: Run the simulator on attention workloads for OPT-13B/QWEN-13B with seq_len in {1K,4K,16K,64K}; compute energy ratio vs 2D-Unfused; assert minimum reduction across configs is within tolerance of 80.5%
verification.paper_cite: Section V-B1: 'reducing energy by 80.5% to 93% compared to 2D-Unfused'

verification.paper_cite: Section V-B-2, Table II average and surrounding text

sub_tasks.requirements: End-to-end attention inference simulation shows the 3D-Flow design achieves ~7.62x average speedup over the 2D-Unfused baseline across evaluated sequence lengths and models.

verification.spec: Run simulator on OPT-13B and Qwen-13B attention for seq_len in {1K,4K,16K,64K} for both 2D-Unfused and 3D-Flow; compute speedup = cycles(2D-Unfused)/cycles(Ours) per config; assert mean speedup satisfies |measured - 7.62| / 7.62 <= 0.25.

sub_tasks.requirements: 3D-Flow achieves ~1.46x average speedup over the FuseMax (2D-Fused) baseline across evaluated sequence lengths and models.

verification.spec: Run simulator with FuseMax dataflow on the 2D 128x128 array and 3D-Flow design across seq_len in {1K,4K,16K,64K}; compute speedup ratio averaged across configs; assert |measured - 1.46| / 1.46 <= 0.25.

sub_tasks.requirements: 3D-Flow achieves ~2.36x average speedup over the Dual-SA (COSA-inspired) baseline.

verification.spec: Simulate Dual-SA (two 128x128 arrays connected by a softmax unit through SRAM) and 3D-Flow on OPT-13B/Qwen-13B for seq_len in {1K,4K,16K,64K}; compute average speedup; assert |measured - 2.36| / 2.36 <= 0.25.

sub_tasks.requirements: 3D-Flow achieves ~1.43x average speedup over the 3D-Base baseline (same 4-tier stacked array but with SRAM-based inter-operator exchange).

verification.spec: Run simulator with 3D-Base mapping (operators mapped to tiers but communicating via SRAM) and with 3D-Flow dataflow across the standard seq_len sweep; assert mean speedup |measured - 1.43| / 1.43 <= 0.25.

sub_tasks.requirements: Average PE utilization of the 3D-Flow architecture across sequence lengths 1K-64K is ~87%.

verification.spec: Run the cycle-accurate simulator for 3D-Flow on attention workloads at seq_len in {1K,4K,8K,16K,32K,64K}; compute PE_utilization = active_PE_cycles / (total_PEs * total_cycles); average across configs; assert |measured - 87| / 87 <= 0.15.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce paper's reported baselines
FuseMax baseline implementation reflects the paper-reported per-PE register count of 10 registers for inter-iteration context switching.

verification.spec: Inspect FuseMax baseline PE configuration; assert per-PE register count == 10

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that FuseMax-style 2D fusion reduces DRAM accesses by 85.5% compared to the 2D-Unfused baseline.

verification.spec: Run the simulator for FuseMax (2D-Fused) and 2D-Unfused baselines on representative attention workloads; compute DRAM_reduction = 1 - (DRAM_accesses_FuseMax / DRAM_accesses_2D-Unfused); assert |measured - 0.855| / 0.855 <= 0.20

sub_tasks.paper_unit: cycles for QK^T on 128x128 2D systolic array

verification.paper_cite: Section V-B1: 'FuseMax cuts DRAM accesses by 85.5%'

sub_tasks.requirements: Code reproduces that FuseMax increases on-chip SRAM traffic by 2.1× relative to 2D-Unfused baseline

verification.paper_cite: Section V-B1: 'increases on-chip SRAM traffic by 2.1x'

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

Reproduce the motivational energy breakdown (Fig. 1) for OPT, comparing operator-fused vs unfused attention at seq_len {1K, 4K, 16K} and reporting MAC / Reg / SRAM / DRAM percentages, demonstrating that on-chip SRAM accesses exceed ~60% of total energy in long-sequence fused workloads.

verification.spec: Run simulator on OPT attention for seq_len in {1K,4K,16K} with both fused and unfused operator modes; extract per-component energy fractions; assert SRAM fraction in the seq_len=16K fused configuration is >= 0.60 (matching paper's >60% claim) and that the qualitative trend (SRAM share grows with seq_len in fused mode) holds.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: % SRAM energy share at long seq_len (fused)

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware / library platforms used in the paper

Code has been implemented that encodes the assumption that a single SRAM access consumes 10-20× more energy than a floating-point multiply-add (FMA), and this ratio is reflected in the Accelergy-based energy model used for evaluation.

verification.spec: Query the Accelergy/energy-table configuration used in evaluation; compute ratio = energy_per_SRAM_access / energy_per_FMA; assert 10 <= ratio <= 24 (i.e., |ratio - 15| / 15 <= 0.6)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and configured such that the on-chip memory bandwidth is set to 8 TB/sec for the 3D-Flow, 2D-Unfused, 2D-Fused, 3D-Base, and Dual-SA configurations in the evaluation.

verification.spec: Parse the simulator config files / accelerator specification table for each baseline; extract on_chip_bw_TB_per_sec; assert all values equal 8.0 with |measured - 8.0| / 8.0 <= 0.01

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and configured such that off-chip DRAM bandwidth is set to 400 GB/sec for all evaluated accelerator configurations (3D-Flow and baselines).

verification.spec: Parse the simulator/Ramulator config files; extract off_chip_bw_GB_per_sec; assert all baselines (Ours, 3D-Base, 2D-Unfused, 2D-Fused, Dual-SA) set to 400 GB/sec with |measured - 400| / 400 <= 0.02

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the 3D-Flow PE array is configured as 128×128 per layer with 4 vertically-stacked layers (total 128×128×4), matching the architectural specification used in evaluation.

verification.spec: Inspect the 3D-Flow accelerator configuration: assert array_dim_x == 128, array_dim_y == 128, num_layers == 4, and that each of the 4 layers implements the correct operator role (Layer 0: QK^T, Layer 1: RowMax/sub, Layer 2: exp/RowSum, Layer 3: PV/scaling)
verification.paper_cite: Section III-A, Section V-A Table I

sub_tasks.requirements: RTL synthesis of the four-tier PE array is performed targeting a TSMC 16nm standard-cell library (or an equivalent open 16nm-class PDK substitute clearly documented), with resulting area/power numbers feeding the energy model.

verification.spec: Inspect synthesis scripts/config in the repo; assert that the target library/tech node is TSMC 16nm (or a documented 16nm-class substitute) and that synthesis reports for the four PE tiers are present.

sub_tasks.requirements: RTL synthesis flow uses Synopsys Design Compiler (or a documented equivalent open synthesis tool such as Yosys with rationale) to produce area, timing, and power reports for the PE tiers.

verification.spec: Check synthesis tcl/scripts; assert presence of Design Compiler invocation (dc_shell) or a clearly documented substitute synthesis tool with generated reports.

sub_tasks.requirements: Simulator integrates Ramulator (v1 or 2.0) to model DRAM access latency and bandwidth for all evaluated configurations.

verification.spec: Importlib check that the simulator module loads and exposes a Ramulator-backed DRAM model; assert calls into Ramulator's request interface are made during a smoke-test run.

sub_tasks.requirements: Energy estimation pipeline invokes Accelergy with per-component activity counts (MAC, register, SRAM, DRAM, 3D-IC links) produced by the simulator.

verification.spec: Run the energy estimation script on a recorded activity trace; assert it shells out to or imports the Accelergy tool and produces a per-component energy breakdown JSON/YAML.

sub_tasks.requirements: Mapping search for baselines (2D-Unfused, 3D-Base) uses a Timeloop-based cost model with a linear pruned search strategy.

verification.spec: Check that the baseline-mapper subdirectory invokes Timeloop (timeloop-mapper or python bindings) with an architecture YAML, and that a pruned/linear search strategy module is present and used.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented for the cycle-accurate 3D-FlashAttention pipeline such that one inner-loop iteration (a d×d QKV tile) completes in 2d cycles when the pipeline is fully filled (where d is the PE array dimension equal to attention head dimension).

verification.spec: Configure 3D-Flow PE array with d=128; run the cycle-accurate simulator on a steady-state inner loop of FlashAttention; measure cycles_per_iteration once pipeline is filled; assert |measured - 2*d| / (2*d) <= 0.10 (i.e., approximately 256 cycles for d=128)

sub_tasks.paper_unit: cycles per FlashAttention inner-loop iteration (for d=128)

sub_tasks.requirements: Cycle-accurate core simulator loop is implemented and tracks per-cycle activity counts for MAC, register reads/writes, SRAM reads/writes, and DRAM reads/writes, exposing them for downstream Accelergy energy estimation.

verification.spec: Run the simulator on a small attention tile (e.g., d=8, two tiles) with a known schedule; assert returned activity-count dict contains keys {mac, reg_read, reg_write, sram_read, sram_write, dram_read, dram_write} with non-negative integers, and that total cycles match an analytical expectation for the toy case.

sub_tasks.requirements: Simulator supports the 4-tier 3D-stacked PE array configuration with per-tier operator assignment (Layer 0: QK^T, Layer 1: rowmax/subtract, Layer 2: exp/rowsum, Layer 3: PV + O-scaling) and models register-to-register TSV forwarding (1.35 pJ/byte) between vertically aligned PEs.

verification.spec: Instantiate the 4-tier 128x128 PE array config; run one inner-loop iteration on a d x d QKV tile; assert (a) intermediate results between tiers are routed through TSV-register channels (not SRAM) as evidenced by zero SRAM access for the inter-operator forwarding events, (b) iteration completes in ~2d cycles once pipeline is full, and (c) TSV energy is accounted at 1.35 pJ/byte.

sub_tasks.requirements: Simulator supports a 2D single-array configuration (128x128) used by the 2D-Unfused and 2D-Fused (FuseMax/FLAT/TileFlow) baselines, with the same on-chip memory (60MB) and bandwidth (8 TB/s on-chip, 400 GB/s off-chip) parameters.

verification.spec: Instantiate the 2D 128x128 configuration; run a small attention workload in both Unfused and FuseMax-style fused modes; assert the configuration reports 60MB on-chip mem, 8 TB/s on-chip BW, 400 GB/s off-chip BW, and that fused mode produces lower DRAM access counts than unfused mode for the same inputs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the cycle-accurate simulator supports a 2D dual-array configuration (128x128x2 PE arrays in 2 clusters) for the Dual-SA baseline (inspired by COSA), including a dedicated softmax unit between the two arrays and the ability to bypass on-chip SRAM for intermediate transfers between QK^T and PV arrays.

verification.spec: Import simulator; instantiate Dual-SA config with array_size=(128,128), clusters=2, on_chip_mem=60MB, on_chip_bw=8TB/s, off_chip_bw=400GB/s; assert config builds and exposes two PE arrays plus a softmax unit; run a short attention workload (seq_len=1K) and assert simulator completes without error and reports separate utilization for each array.
verification.paper_cite: Section V-A Baselines (3) Dual-SA; Table I

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the synthesized area of a single 128x128 PE array layer is approximately 80 mm^2 in TSMC 16nm.

verification.spec: Synthesize the RTL of a single 128x128 PE array layer with Synopsys Design Compiler on TSMC 16nm (or use the calibrated area model in the repo); extract total area; assert |area_mm2 - 80| / 80 <= 0.25.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the single-layer peak power P_layer for a 128x128 PE array at peak activity (P_PE = 200 µW per PE) is approximately 3.3 W.

verification.spec: Compute P_layer = 128*128*P_PE with P_PE=200e-6 W; assert |P_layer - 3.3| / 3.3 <= 0.10.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the four-layer 3D stack total power P_total is approximately 13.1 W and the per-layer power density ρ is approximately 41 W/cm^2.

verification.spec: Compute P_total = 4 * P_layer and rho = P_layer / (A_cm2); assert |P_total - 13.1| / 13.1 <= 0.10 and |rho - 41| / 41 <= 0.15.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the junction temperature T_j at 25°C ambient using R_θJA ≈ 2.5 K/W is approximately 83°C for the four-layer stack.

verification.spec: Compute T_j = 25 + P_total * R_thetaJA with P_total=13.1 W, R_thetaJA=2.5 K/W; assert |T_j - 83| / 83 <= 0.10.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the attention energy consumption on OPT-13B is reproduced for all 7 designs (2D-Unfused, FLAT, TileFlow, FuseMax, Dual-SA, 3D-Base, Ours) at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, and 64K using the cycle-accurate simulator + Ramulator + Accelergy stack.

verification.spec: Run simulator for each (design, seq_len) pair on OPT-13B attention layers; collect per-design energy at each seq_len; verify that Ours achieves 80.5%-93% reduction vs 2D-Unfused and 46.8% average reduction vs 3D-Base; assert relative orderings match paper Fig. 5 and that energy reduction of Ours vs 2D-Unfused averaged across seq_lens is within tolerance of 0.87 (midpoint).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the attention energy consumption on Qwen-13B (GQA) is reproduced for all 7 designs at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, and 64K.

verification.spec: Run simulator for each (design, seq_len) pair on Qwen-13B attention layers (GQA); verify Ours achieves significant reduction vs all baselines matching Fig. 5; assert energy of Ours normalized to 2D-Unfused averaged across seq_lens is within tolerance of 0.13 (i.e. ~87% reduction).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the energy results in Figure 5 are normalized to the 2D-Unfused baseline (per (model, seq_len)) and the normalization procedure matches the paper's presentation.

verification.spec: Call the normalization function on the raw energy table; assert that for every (model, seq_len) the 2D-Unfused entry equals 1.0 and all other designs are expressed as ratios; assert ratio for Ours is in [0.07, 0.20] across all (model, seq_len) entries matching Fig. 5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the inference speedup on OPT-13B is reproduced for all 7 designs at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, and 64K, with Ours achieving ~7.62x average speedup over 2D-Unfused.

verification.spec: Run simulator for each (design, seq_len) on OPT-13B; collect cycles; compute speedup = cycles_2D-Unfused / cycles_design; assert mean speedup of Ours vs 2D-Unfused across seq_lens satisfies |measured - 7.62| / 7.62 <= 0.20, and Ours vs FuseMax ~1.46x, Ours vs Dual-SA ~2.36x, Ours vs 3D-Base ~1.43x within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the inference speedup on Qwen-13B (GQA) is reproduced for all 7 designs at sequence lengths 1K, 4K, 16K, and 64K.

verification.spec: Run simulator for each (design, seq_len) on Qwen-13B; compute speedup normalized to 2D-Unfused; assert mean speedup of Ours across seq_lens is within tolerance of 7.62x and relative ordering matches Fig. 7.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that speedup results in Figure 7 are normalized to the 2D-Unfused baseline (per (model, seq_len)) and the normalization procedure matches the paper's presentation.

verification.spec: Call the normalization function on raw cycle counts; assert 2D-Unfused entries equal 1.0 in the normalized table; assert Ours entries are in [1.4, 8.5] for all (model, seq_len) consistent with the 1.4x-7.6x claim.

sub_tasks.requirements: Hyperparameters that determine reproducibility
Package thermal resistance R_theta_JA is set to approximately 2.5 K/W (conservative assumption from TI ref).

verification.spec: Inspect thermal model config; assert R_theta_JA == 2.5 K/W (within 5%)

sub_tasks.requirements: Energy model uses Z-axis (inter-tier register-to-register) data transfer energy of 1.35 pJ/byte.

verification.spec: Inspect Accelergy/energy-model config; assert tsv_z_axis_energy_per_byte == 1.35 pJ/byte (within 5%)
